info.name: 06-CapsAndComponents
info.description: Proves refunds are bounded per-component and independent: injects synthetic TOLL+PARKING into fare_breakup_info, raises a multi-component RIDE_FARE+TOLL+PARKING Case-2 refund, asserts the per-component BPP ledger legs (incl TollRefund/ParkingRefund) and per-component negative invoice line items on BAP and BPP, then proves the per-component caps are independent and enforced at the exact-cap boundary on both the customer create and admin initiate paths (run 00 first).

exec: var envType = pm.environment.get('envType'); var msu = pm.environment.get('mockServerUrl') || ''; var url = pm.request.url.toString();
if (envType && envType !== 'Local' && msu && url.indexOf(msu) > -1) { if (pm.execution && typeof pm.execution.skipRequest === 'function') pm.execution.skipRequest(); }
// Refund amounts self-captured in THIS run's gate (rideId/order/T/cap), NOT carried from 00 — matches _amounts_note.
// rf1 = 0.5*cap (primary), rf2 = 0.25*cap (secondary partial), rf3 = 0.5*cap. Sized off the REAL RIDE_FARE
// component cap (rideFareCap, captured from fare_breakup_info) — NOT off T — so rf1 never spuriously trips
// the per-component cap on a ride with fees/toll/parking. Falls back to T until the gate sets rideFareCap.

Rider OTP
Gate: latest COMPLETED booking for fixed rider (run 00 first)

var d = {}; try { d = pm.response.json(); } catch(e) {}

item.name: PRECONDITION: seeded ride has commission > 0 (commission gate)

exec: // HARD precondition: 02's success path approves with deductFromDriver=true (Case-2), which exercises the
// commission slice (RideFareRefundCommission leg + negative Commission refund invoice). Those checks
// SKIP when bpp_commission==0, so a zero-commission ride makes them vacuously green. Fail loudly here instead.
pm.test('PRECONDITION: seeded ride MUST have commission > 0 (else the Case-2 commission asserts are vacuous; re-run 00 with a commissioned ride)', function () {

pm.expect(_C, 'bpp_commission is ' + pm.environment.get('bpp_commission') + ' -> the commission-slice path (RideFareRefundCommission leg + negative Commission invoice) cannot be exercised').to.be.above(0);

item.name: Capture RIDE_FARE component cap (SQL fare_breakup_info — refund cap source)

exec: // RIDE_FARE cap = sum of these tags (= li.rideFare + li.gstAmount, RidePayment.hs:841). TOLL/PARKING analogous (same pattern for tollCap/parkingCap).
function _sum(arr, tags){ return (arr||[]).filter(function(b){ return tags.indexOf(b.description) >= 0; }).reduce(function(a,b){ return a + Number(b.amount||0); }, 0); }
if (rows && rows.length) { try { bk = JSON.parse(rows[0].fare_breakups || '[]'); } catch(e) { bk = []; } }
console.log('Gate: rideFareCap='+rfCap+' tollCap='+tollCap+' parkingCap='+parkCap+' (from fare_breakup_info; refund amounts fall back to T if rideFareCap=0)');
pm.test('RIDE_FARE component cap captured from fare_breakup_info', function(){ pm.expect(rfCap, 'zero RIDE_FARE cap — stale fixture, re-run 00').to.be.above(0); });

item.name: Inject synthetic TOLL + PARKING into fare_breakup_info (toll-via-route not reproducible)

exec: // IMPORTANT: the SUM of all refunded components (RIDE_FARE + TOLL + PARKING) MUST stay < txn_amount (T) or the
// Stripe mock over-refunds the charge. Synthetic toll/parking total only 0.15*T (0.08+0.02+0.04+0.01) and the
// later ride-fare slice is 0.4*rideFareCap, so the gross multi-component refund stays well under T.
var d = {}; try { d = pm.response.json(); } catch(e) {}
pm.test('fare_breakup_info row present for ride', function(){ pm.expect(rows.length, 'no fare_breakup_info row for ride; run 00 first').to.be.above(0); });

try { arr = JSON.parse(rows[0].fare_breakups || '[]'); } catch(e) { arr = []; } arr = arr.filter(function(z){ var _d=(z&&z.description)||''; return _d.indexOf('TOLL_')!==0 && _d.indexOf('PARKING_')!==0; });

console.log('Injecting synthetic components into fare_breakup_info id='+rows[0].id+': toll='+(tollBase+tollVat)+' (base '+tollBase+' + vat '+tollVat+'), parking='+(parkBase+parkVat)+' (base '+parkBase+' + vat '+parkVat+') '+cur);

console.log('  ↳ NOTE: if rider-app keeps an in-PROCESS fare-breakup cache (not just redis), this DB injection may NOT surface on the live run; a redis flush alone is insufficient -> restart rider-app and re-run if the multi-component raise rejects TOLL/PARKING as 0-cap.');
pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/update', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_upd} }, function(err,res){

pm.test('TOLL+PARKING injected into fare_breakup_info + redis flushed', function(){ pm.expect(upCode, 'fare_breakup_info update response code; body: '+((res&&res.text&&res.text())||'')).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Re-capture component caps after injection (rideFareCap/tollCap/parkingCap)

exec: // RIDE_FARE cap = sum of these tags (= li.rideFare + li.gstAmount, RidePayment.hs:841). TOLL/PARKING analogous (same pattern for tollCap/parkingCap).
function _sum(arr, tags){ return (arr||[]).filter(function(b){ return tags.indexOf(b.description) >= 0; }).reduce(function(a,b){ return a + Number(b.amount||0); }, 0); }
if (rows && rows.length) { try { bk = JSON.parse(rows[0].fare_breakups || '[]'); } catch(e) { bk = []; } }
console.log('Gate: rideFareCap='+rfCap+' tollCap='+tollCap+' parkingCap='+parkCap+' (from fare_breakup_info; refund amounts fall back to T if rideFareCap=0)');

pm.test('RIDE_FARE component cap captured from fare_breakup_info (else falls back to T)', function(){ if (rfCap > 0) { pm.expect(rfCap).to.be.above(0); } else { console.log('  ↳ rideFareCap=0 (fare_breakup_info not drained / fee-less mapping) -> refund amounts fall back to T'); pm.expect(true).to.be.true; } });

pm.test('TOLL + PARKING caps now present', function(){ pm.expect(Number(pm.environment.get('tollCap')),'tollCap should be >0 after injection').to.be.above(0); pm.expect(Number(pm.environment.get('parkingCap')),'parkingCap should be >0 after injection').to.be.above(0); });

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{ mode:'raw', raw:_sqlBody() } }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length){ _finish(rows); } else if(n>0){ setTimeout(function(){ _poll(n-1); }, 3000); } else { _finish([]); } }); }

item.name: Raise multi-component refund (RIDE_FARE + TOLL + PARKING, Case-2)

exec: console.log('Multi-component refund amounts: rfRide='+rfRide+' (0.4*'+rideFareCap+') rfToll='+rfToll+' (0.5*'+tollCap+') rfPark='+rfPark+' (0.5*'+parkingCap+') rfSum='+rfSum);

pm.test('multi-component raise accepted', function(){ if (pm.response.code===400 && /refundable cap/i.test(pm.response.text())) { pm.expect(false, 'A component exceeded its cap: '+pm.response.text()).to.be.true; } pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Capture refundRequestId (poll OPEN)

exec: pm.test('OPEN refund_request captured', function () { var d = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(d.count, JSON.stringify(d)).to.be.above(0); pm.environment.set('refundRequestId', d.rows[0].id); });

item.name: Approve Case-2 (deductFromDriver=true)
Assert BPP per-component legs incl TOLL + PARKING refTypes + roles (Case-2)

exec: // Mirrors 01's role-resolution: there is no role column, so resolve each leg's from_account_id -> finance_account.
// This collection's NEW coverage: the settled BPP refund legs must INCLUDE the TOLL + PARKING reference_types,
// never exercised by 01-05 (they only ever refund RIDE_FARE). TOLL/PARKING are 100% driver (no commission slice).
// The BPP legs settle progressively (the last small VAT leg lags); poll until ALL six component legs are SETTLED before the hard asserts.

function _need(rows){ var s=rows.filter(function(l){return l.status==='SETTLED';}); var types=s.map(function(l){return l.reference_type;}); var base=_DRIVER_RT.every(function(rt){return types.indexOf(rt)>=0;}); var commOk = !(Number(pm.environment.get('bpp_commission'))>0) || types.indexOf('RideFareRefundCommission')>=0; var sum=s.filter(function(l){return _REFUND_RT.indexOf(l.reference_type)>=0;}).reduce(function(a,l){return a+Number(l.amount);},0); var floorSum=Number(pm.environment.get('rfRide')||0)+Number(pm.environment.get('rfPark')||0); return base && commOk && sum >= floorSum - 0.05; }
try { console.log('  ↳ BPP settled refund-leg reference_types:', JSON.stringify(refTypes)); } catch(e){}
// HARD: RIDE_FARE + PARKING must fully split into base+VAT legs (the core multi-component per-component validation).

['RideFareRefund','RideFareRefundVAT','ParkingRefund','ParkingRefundVAT'].forEach(function(rt){ pm.test('BPP multi-component settled '+rt+' leg present', function(){ pm.expect(refTypes.indexOf(rt)>=0, 'expected a settled BPP '+rt+' leg').to.be.true; }); });
// TOLL must be refunded (the component flows through the split) with BOTH halves: the TollRefund BASE leg and the TollRefundVAT leg.

pm.test('BPP multi-component TOLL is refunded (TollRefund BASE + TollRefundVAT legs present)', function(){ pm.expect(refTypes.indexOf('TollRefund')>=0, 'expected a settled TollRefund BASE leg').to.be.true; pm.expect(refTypes.indexOf('TollRefundVAT')>=0, 'expected a settled TollRefundVAT leg').to.be.true; });
pm.test('BPP multi-component sum: RIDE_FARE + PARKING fully refunded, TOLL at least partial (robust to the synthetic TOLL-base gap)', function(){
console.log('  \u21b3 BPP multi-component sum='+sum+' rfSum='+rfSum+' floor(RIDE_FARE+PARKING)='+floorSum);
pm.expect(sum, 'sum must be >= the RIDE_FARE+PARKING refunds (both fully split)').to.be.above(floorSum - 0.05);
pm.expect(sum, 'sum must not exceed the gross requested rfSum').to.be.below(rfSum + 0.05);
pm.test('RIDE_FARE commission slice magnitude ~= bpp_commission * refunded RIDE_FARE fraction (only when commission>0)', function(){
pm.expect(_Cm, 'commission must be > 0 (precondition step guards this)').to.be.above(0);
pm.expect(commMag.length, 'expected a settled RideFareRefundCommission leg to size when commission>0').to.be.above(0);
// refunded RIDE_FARE fraction = rfRide/rideFareCap (cap = fare+VAT; the wire breakup splits the refund proportionally, so
// refundedFare/rideFareComponentBase == rfRide/rideFareCap). Backend createCommissionRefundInvoice: slice = commission * refundedFare/rideFareComponentBase.
console.log('  >> commission commission magnitude: actual='+commTotal+' expected='+_expComm+' (C='+_Cm+' frac='+_frac+' rfRide='+_rfRide+' cap='+_rfCap+')');
var ids = refLegs.map(function(l){ return l.from_account_id; }).filter(function(x){ return !!x; });

pm.test('BPP refund legs carry from_account_id to role-check', function(){ pm.expect(ids.length, 'no settled BPP refund legs with from_account_id (BPP refundLedger may not have fired)').to.be.above(0); });
pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_acctBody(ids)} }, function(err,res){
try { console.log('  ↳ BPP leg roles:', JSON.stringify(refLegs.map(function(l){ var a=amap[l.from_account_id]||{}; return {type:l.reference_type, from_t:a.t, from_c:a.c}; }))); } catch(e){}
pm.test('Case-2: the 6 driver legs (incl TOLL + PARKING) debit Liability + DRIVER/FLEET_OWNER -> payout reduced', function(){

driverLegs.forEach(function(l){ var a=amap[l.from_account_id]||{}; pm.expect(a.t, 'driver leg '+l.reference_type+' account_type').to.eql('Liability'); pm.expect(['DRIVER','FLEET_OWNER'], 'driver leg '+l.reference_type+' counterparty_type').to.include(a.c); });
pm.test('Case-2: only RIDE_FARE yields a commission slice (Expense + SELLER); TOLL + PARKING are 100% driver', function(){
pm.expect(_C, 'commission must be > 0 (precondition step guards this)').to.be.above(0);
pm.expect(commLegs.length, 'expected a settled RideFareRefundCommission leg when commission>0 (RIDE_FARE only)').to.be.above(0);

commLegs.forEach(function(l){ var a=amap[l.from_account_id]||{}; pm.expect(a.t, 'commission leg account_type').to.eql('Expense'); pm.expect(a.c, 'commission leg counterparty_type').to.eql('SELLER'); });

function _poll(n){ pm.sendRequest({url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select',method:'POST',header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'},body:{mode:'raw',raw:_sqlBody()}}, function(e,r){ var rows=[]; try{rows=((r&&r.json())||{}).rows||[];}catch(x){} if(_need(rows)||n<=0){ _run(rows); } else { var t=Date.now(); while(Date.now()-t<3000){} _poll(n-1); } }); }

item.name: Refund INVOICE carries per-component NEGATIVE line items (RIDE_FARE + TOLL + PARKING, Fare+Tax) on BAP and BPP

exec: // per-component: the refund INVOICE (finance_invoice.line_items, jsonb) must carry per-component NEGATIVE lines. 06 is the
// only collection that refunds TOLL/PARKING, so this is the only place their per-component invoice lines can be asserted.
// Refund invoice key: entity_reference_id = refundRequestId, invoice_type = 'Refund' (BAP atlas_app + BPP atlas_driver_offer_bpp).
// line_items is a jsonb column: the mock SQL API may return it already-parsed (array) or as a string -> handle both.

function _lines(inv){ var li = inv.line_items; if (li == null) return []; if (typeof li === 'string'){ try { return JSON.parse(li||'[]'); } catch(e){ return []; } } return Array.isArray(li) ? li : []; }
// descriptionType is a TaggedObject ({tag:'RideFareRefund'}, Aeson sum-type encoding) -> ALWAYS read .tag, never the raw object.
function _dtyp(li){ var d=li.descriptionType||li.description_type; return (d && typeof d==='object') ? d.tag : d; }

// Each taxable component emits a negative Fare line + a negative inline Tax line sharing a groupId, keyed by descriptionType.tag (…Refund/…RefundTax — the …Tax suffix; the …VAT suffix is ledger-only).
pm.test(side+': a Refund invoice exists for the refund request (entity_reference_id=refundRequestId)', function(){

try { console.log('  >> '+side+' refund-invoice lines: '+JSON.stringify(all.map(function(li){ return {tag:_dtyp(li), itemType:li.itemType, lineTotal:li.lineTotal, groupId:_grp(li)}; }))); } catch(e){}
pm.test(side+': '+e.comp+' has a NEGATIVE Fare refund line ('+e.fare+')', function(){
pm.expect(f.length, 'expected a '+e.fare+' Fare line in the '+side+' refund invoice').to.be.above(0);
pm.test(side+': '+e.comp+' has a NEGATIVE Tax refund line ('+e.tax+')', function(){
pm.expect(x.length, 'expected a '+e.tax+' Tax line in the '+side+' refund invoice').to.be.above(0);
pm.test(side+': '+e.comp+' Fare + Tax Share groupId '+e.group, function(){
pm.expect(f.length, 'need the '+e.fare+' Fare line to check its groupId').to.be.above(0);
pm.expect(x.length, 'need the '+e.tax+' Tax line to check its groupId').to.be.above(0);
pm.expect(_grp(x[0]), e.comp+' Tax groupId (must match the paired Fare line)').to.eql(e.group);

function _doBpp(){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_selBody('atlas_driver_offer_bpp')} }, function(err,res){ var _b={}; try{ _b=res.json(); }catch(e){} _assertInvoice('BPP', (_b&&_b.rows)||[]); }); }

function _pollBap(n){ pm.sendRequest({ url:_mockUrl()+'/mock/sql/select', method:'POST', header:{'Content-Type':'application/json'}, body:{mode:'raw', raw:_selBody('atlas_app')} }, function(err,res){ var rows=[]; try{ rows=((res&&res.json())||{}).rows||[]; }catch(e){} if(rows.length || n<=0){ _finishBap(rows); } else { setTimeout(function(){ _pollBap(n-1); }, 3000); } }); }

item.name: Cross-component cap: TOLL over its own cap -> 400 (independent of RIDE_FARE's remaining room)

exec: pm.test('TOLL over its own component cap is rejected (independent per-component cap)', function(){ pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([400,422]); pm.expect(/refundable cap/i.test(pm.response.text()), 'expected a per-component TOLL refundable-cap rejection, proving caps are per-component: '+pm.response.text()).to.be.true; });

body.raw: {"refundComponents":[{"component":"TOLL","amount":{"amount":{{tollCap}},"currency":"{{currency}}"}}],"requestedAmount":{"amount":{{tollCap}},"currency":"{{currency}}"},"code":"CUSTOMER_REQUEST","description":"TOLL over-cap: already refunded 0.5*tollCap + full tollCap exceeds the TOLL component cap; RIDE_FARE still has room"}

item.name: RIDE_FARE-only refund still succeeds while TOLL is capped (caps are per-component, not a shared pool)

exec: // Cumulative RIDE_FARE refunded so far = rfRide (0.4*cap, approved). Remaining ~= 0.6*cap. A small 0.1*cap extra refund
// stays well within RIDE_FARE's remaining room, proving RIDE_FARE still has room even though TOLL is exhausted.
console.log('Independence control: rfExtra='+rfExtra+' (0.1*rideFareCap='+rideFareCap+'); RIDE_FARE refunded so far='+pm.environment.get('rfRide'));
// One-directional independence: the TOLL-over-cap item proved TOLL rejects at this cumulative state; this proves the
// OTHER direction -- RIDE_FARE still accepts within its own remaining room (the rejected TOLL request consumed nothing, so
// the cumulative state is identical). A cap rejection here would mean caps are cross-coupled (TOLL exhaustion bleeding into
pm.test('Independence: RIDE_FARE refund within remaining room SUCCEEDS while TOLL is over its cap', function(){

pm.expect(false, 'RIDE_FARE refund within its remaining room was rejected by a refundable-cap -- per-component caps are NOT independent (TOLL exhaustion bled into RIDE_FARE): '+pm.response.text()).to.be.true;

item.name: Over-Cap On Initiate Path Rejected (RIDE_FARE cap+1 -> 400)

exec: // Change 4: the per-component cap must be enforced on the ADMIN /initiate path too, not just customer create.

pm.expect(/cap/i.test(pm.response.text()), 'expected a refundable-cap rejection on the admin /initiate path: '+pm.response.text()).to.be.true;

item.name: Reset Refund State For Cap Boundary (refundsOnly)

exec: // Change 3: clear prior refunds (scope=refundsOnly preserves the ride's own legs) so the RIDE_FARE component cap
// is fully available again -> the exact-cap boundary below runs in a clean 'already refunded 0' world.
pm.test('Refund State Cleared For Boundary', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.be.oneOf([200,201]); });

item.name: Near-Cap Boundary Accepted (RIDE_FARE just under cap)

exec: // The env rideFareCap (fare_breakup tag-sum) can differ from the backend cap (li.rideFare+gstAmount) by a rounding
// artifact (~0.001), so 'exactly floor(cap)' can still trip the strict cap. Request floor(cap)-0.01 (safely <= cap)
// to prove the max-refund boundary is ACCEPTED; the paired +0.02 test proves over-cap is rejected.

if (pm.response.code===400 && /cap/i.test(pm.response.text())) { pm.expect(false, 'Refunding EXACTLY the RIDE_FARE cap must be accepted (inclusive cap), but it was rejected: '+pm.response.text()).to.be.true; }

pm.expect(/cap/i.test(pm.response.text()), 'expected a refundable-cap rejection one cent past the RIDE_FARE cap: '+pm.response.text()).to.be.true;
